health.mental_health: Increased anxiety and occasional insomnia tied to long-distance relationship stress, relocation uncertainty, and worries about COPD flare-ups while traveling; uses breathing exercises and is considering short-term therapy.
recent_visits: Pulmonology follow-up at Massachusetts General Hospital (spirometry review, inhaler technique check) - 6 weeks ago
Primary care visit at Beth Israel Deaconess for blood pressure medication adjustment - 3 months ago
Urgent care visit in Somerville for COPD exacerbation after a winter respiratory infection (short prednisone taper prescribed) - 5 months ago
Pharmacy consult for smoking-cessation support and nicotine lozenges (returned to occasional use during stress) - 2 months ago

finance.debt: Still carrying about $14,000 on a credit card after helping an adult niece with emergency rent and legal fees; also has a remaining car loan balance and is trying to pay down debt before relocating.
investments: High-yield savings account earmarked for relocation costs
recent_transactions: Paid $1,250 deposit for a Boston-area apartment lease extension (month-to-month) to keep flexibility for relocation
Purchased two round-trip flights to visit partner (one rescheduled with change fee due to a COPD flare)
Paid $420 out-of-pocket for inhalers after insurance deductible reset
Consultation fee ($300) for a relocation-focused financial planner session
Monthly transfer of $350 to a savings bucket labeled "Move/First+Last"

relationships: Long-distance partner: Marcus O'Neill (lives in Raleigh, North Carolina; discussing Zainab relocating within the next year)
Close friend and former colleague: Elena Ruiz (Boston; confidante about relationship and health)
Adult niece: Asha Banda (Providence; intermittent financial support and boundary-setting tensions)
Older sister: Mariam Banda (Chicago; frequent calls, mixed feelings about Zainab moving farther away)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps a low public profile; mostly follows local politics and arts accounts on X and Instagram, occasionally shares links to her published pieces; avoids posting about her relationship or health and uses a private Facebook group for neighborhood updates.
private_conversations: Negotiating whether to move to Raleigh versus having Marcus eventually move to Boston, including concerns about giving up her newsroom role
Discussions about her COPD and fear of being seen as "less reliable" at work if colleagues find out
Arguments with her niece about money and repayment expectations
Conversations with Marcus about intimacy challenges and sleeping issues during visits

schedule.work_hours: Typically 9:30am–6:30pm weekdays with frequent evening community meetings and occasional weekend breaking-news shifts; deadline-heavy with late nights on Tuesdays and Thursdays.
regular_appointments: Pulmonary rehab exercise class (Mondays and Wednesdays, 5:30pm)
Therapy intake call (biweekly, Thursday lunch hour; recently scheduled but not yet consistent)
Blood pressure check-in at pharmacy kiosk (first Saturday of each month)
Weekly video call with Marcus (Sunday evenings)
upcoming_events: Three-day reporting trip to Springfield for a housing-court series (next month)
Weekend visit to Raleigh to tour neighborhoods and meet Marcus's adult daughter (in 6 weeks)
Pulmonology follow-up with repeat spirometry (in 2 months)
Lease decision deadline for her Boston apartment (end of next quarter)
schedule.daily_routine: Early morning inhalers and a short walk if air quality is good; newsroom by mid-morning, interviews and city meetings in afternoons, writing/editing at night; uses a humidifier and tracks pollen/air-quality before exercising; ends most days with a video call or text check-in with Marcus.

professional.company: Metro desk at The Boston Herald (local daily newspaper)
projects: Investigative series on eviction filings and the impact of rent hikes across Greater Boston
Profiles of neighborhood health clinics and gaps in respiratory-care access
Coverage of City Council debates on MBTA funding and service reliability
Weekend feature pitch: "What it costs to move out of Massachusetts" tied to relocation trends
professional.performance_issues: Managing fatigue and occasional shortness of breath has led to a few last-minute reassigned evening events; she is also distracted by relocation planning and has asked her editor for more predictable scheduling, which she worries could affect future assignments.
workplace_relationships: Editor: Tom Kelleher (supportive but deadline-driven; expects quick turnarounds)
Investigations partner: Priya Desai (collaborative, occasionally frustrated when Zainab declines late-night meetings)
City Hall source relationships: a few trusted aides who prefer off-the-record calls and discreet meeting locations

hobbies: Long walks along the Emerald Necklace (weather and air-quality permitting)
Listening to public radio and investigative podcasts
Cooking West African-inspired stews and experimenting with low-sodium recipes
Attending local author talks at the Boston Public Library
Photography of neighborhood street scenes for personal projects
habits: Uses inhalers on a strict schedule and keeps a rescue inhaler in every bag
Tracks air quality and avoids heavy outdoor activity on poor-AQI days
Occasional nicotine lozenge use during high-stress periods (keeps it private)
Late-night writing and caffeine on deadline days, followed by sleep disruption
Regular video calls and shared calendar planning with her long-distance partner
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives alone in a rent-stabilized one-bedroom in Jamaica Plain; keeping the lease month-to-month while exploring a potential move to Raleigh to be closer to her partner, with a contingency plan to sublet if the relocation happens quickly.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly home-cooked meals; aims for low-sodium and higher-protein to support breathing and stamina; limits alcohol due to sleep and reflux; keeps ginger tea and throat lozenges on hand during winter.
